problem: helix-cli lacks a README, automated npm publish CI, and artifact retrieval commands. Version drift exists between package.json (1.2.0) and the hardcoded --version output (0.1.0). No .github/workflows directory exists. No test files exist.

files.why: Package metadata, npm publish config (name: @projectxinnovation/helix-cli, version: 1.2.0, bin: dist/index.js, files: [dist], scripts: build/typecheck, engines: node>=18). Defines the publishable surface.
TypeScript build config. Compiles src/ to dist/ with ES2022 target, Node16 module resolution, strict mode, declarations enabled.
Ignores node_modules/ and dist/. dist is not committed but is the npm publish target (files: [dist]).

CLI entrypoint (shebang #!/usr/bin/env node). Defines top-level command router: login, inspect, comments, --version. Hardcodes version string '0.1.0' which drifts from package.json 1.2.0. This is where new 'artifacts' command would be registered.
Auth/config layer. Reads from env vars (HELIX_API_KEY, HELIX_URL, etc.) or ~/.hlx/config.json. Defines HxConfig type (apiKey, url). Used by all authenticated commands.

HTTP client with retry/backoff. hxFetch() sends requests to Helix backend with bearer or X-API-Key auth. Base path defaults to /api/inspect. Supports configurable basePath. This is the HTTP layer new artifact commands would use.
Repository resolution helper. Fetches repo list from backend and resolves by ID, name, or partial match. Used by inspect subcommands.
Login flow: browser-based OAuth callback on localhost or --manual API key entry. Saves config to ~/.hlx/config.json.
Inspect command router: repos, db, logs, api subcommands. Each requires --repo flag. Hand-rolled arg parsing (getFlag, getPositionalArgs).
Lists available repos with inspection types.
Queries logs via POST /{repoId}/logs with optional limit.
Comments command router: list, post subcommands. Resolves ticket ID from --ticket flag or HELIX_TICKET_ID env var.
Lists ticket comments via GET /api/tickets/{ticketId}/comments. Supports --helix-only and --since filters.
Posts comment to ticket via POST /api/tickets/{ticketId}/comments.

facts: Package name is @projectxinnovation/helix-cli, scoped npm package.
package.json version is 1.2.0 but src/index.ts --version outputs hardcoded '0.1.0' — confirmed version drift.
npm bin entry is 'hlx' pointing to 'dist/index.js'.
package.json files array is ['dist'] — only dist/ is included in published package.
dist/ is gitignored; must be built before publish (npm run build → tsc).
Typecheck passes cleanly (npm run typecheck exits 0).
No test files exist anywhere in the repo (no *.test.*, no *.spec.*).
No .github/ directory exists — no CI workflows at all.
No README.md exists at repo root.
Only devDependencies: @types/node ^25.5.0 and typescript ^6.0.2. No runtime dependencies — CLI uses only Node builtins and native fetch.
No ORM, no database schema, no migrations — not applicable to this repo.
Auth config reads env vars: HELIX_API_KEY/HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN/HELIX_INSPECT_API_KEY and HELIX_URL/HELIX_INSPECT_BASE_URL/HELIX_INSPECT_URL.
Config file stored at ~/.hlx/config.json with shape {apiKey, url}.
HTTP client supports two auth modes: hxi_* prefix → X-API-Key header; otherwise → Bearer token.
hxFetch default basePath is /api/inspect; comments use basePath /api. New artifact commands will need to determine appropriate basePath.
CLI uses hand-rolled arg parsing (no commander/yargs dependency). Command routing is a switch statement in src/index.ts.
Existing command structure: login, inspect (repos/db/logs/api), comments (list/post), --version.
No existing 'artifacts' command or any artifact-retrieval logic.
No package-lock.json lockfileVersion checked — file exists at repo root.
Node engine requirement is >=18 (needed for native fetch).
unknowns: Whether the @projectxinnovation/helix-cli npm package is currently published and what version is live on npm.
Whether artifact retrieval should go through the Helix backend API (like inspect/comments do) or directly to GitHub/Vercel APIs. The existing pattern is: CLI → Helix backend → external service.
What Helix backend endpoints exist (or need to exist) for artifact retrieval by ticket ID or run ID.
What GitHub repo(s) and Vercel project(s) artifact retrieval should target, and what credentials/tokens are needed.
Whether the Helix backend already has any artifact-related API routes.
What npm publish access model is used (public vs restricted) and what npm token/secret name is expected in GitHub Actions.
Whether publish should trigger on every push to main or only on version bumps — ticket leaves this as an open decision.
No runtime inspection manifest available — cannot verify production API behavior or existing artifact endpoints.
